title: Ports, Immutable Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in explicitly owned crawler component graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, render, audit, storage, and report dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Create readonly branded identities only after unknown runtime values pass current validation and normalization.
Assign each Promise a caller, settlement path, concurrency slot, cancellation rule, error destination, and shutdown consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Directed Ports
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 4385 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove port direction, admitted values, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, and handle-clean lifecycle; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, render, audit, storage, and report dependencies through narrow typed ports.
